mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes corporate profit margins and consumer purchasing power, leading to a general decline in equity valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the short-to-medium term.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth over any 3-year rolling window, or average returns are positive.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and broader economic slowdown, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous and significant decline in both retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates a broad-based weakening of consumer demand and investment, signaling an impending recession and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where both RSAFS and HOUST show significant 3-month declines as per the trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of aggressive monetary tightening, rising federal funds rates increase the cost of capital and reduce liquidity, which typically leads to a contraction in equity valuations as future earnings are discounted more heavily, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show positive performance in more than 55% of triggered instances during identified monetary tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Robust and sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strong underlying economic activity and healthy corporate profitability, which provides a supportive environment for higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with INDPRO month-over-month growth over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals a weakening economic expansion, which translates into lower corporate earnings expectations and subsequently negative S&P 500 forward returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where GDPC1 quarterly growth falls below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment (UNRATE) is low while inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising, it signals an overheating economy that will likely prompt aggressive monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as interest rates rise and growth slows.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in more than 45% of instances where UNRATE is below 5.0% and CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong consumer spending, as indicated by robust retail sales (RSAFS) growth, corporate revenues and profits are expected to grow, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns show no statistically significant positive performance in periods where RSAFS 3-month growth exceeds 1.5%.
